headline_pain.summary: Can't measure who's actually using it

pain_quotes.quote: the first question we had was, well, how many people are using it? And that has proven to be a monumental undertaking. That is, that is still pretty much unanswered.
we have people who have like years of classes that they've taken and certifications they may have. Would you guys be able to port that over to show in their historical transcript?
pain_quotes.attribution: Savannah DeCuir · Keesler Federal Credit Union · 662 employees

pain_quotes.quote: this is almost double what we pay currently. And so it's a shock. only 30 courses for 23,000 a year. I thought that with your partnership, we would be able to get a lower cost on the content.
we're trying to like move and expand our training and our capabilities in the LMS space. And I don't think that ispring can do that.
majority of it is in Salesforce. So having the ability to cross training along with the reporting and seeing the correlation would be a nice clutch for us.
I keep getting told it's impossible. And I know that it's not possible.
we're not going to have a 10 person team running this effort. It's going to be probably one person and part of one person or part of a bunch of people.
Our LMS right now that we have is kind of lacking a lot of the more modernization that we're looking for. There's not one piece of AI. It's kind of concerning at this point in time.
pain_quotes.attribution: Annette DiGuiseppi · PKF O'Connor Davies · 1500 employees

pain_quotes.quote: right now, the learning onboarding, continuation, training, compliance, things like that. It's not really in a set platform that's trackable and manageable. It's more like spreadsheet based and email confirmations.
Our library is very limited, which is why I'm looking for something that people don't feel like they've already watched this or seen this before.
pain_quotes.attribution: Suzanne Hessing · Eastern Warehouse Distributors · 407 employees

decision_criteria.criterion: Reporting beyond completion (active users, drop-off, retention)

language_they_use: endless options to continue to improve
headline_ready_quotes: I keep getting told it's impossible. And I know that it's not possible.
we don't have a learning management system, of any sort.
this platform would provide us endless options to continue to improve and build out for the next several years.
it's like a huge upgrade to the nothing that we have.

competitive_triggers.switch_reason: doesn't scale beyond quizzes, no robust learner experience tracking
engagement low without top-down push, no Japanese, content-bundle pricing
clunky authoring, vendor explicitly not investing in improvements
closed library, no external content allowed, long-tenured learners feel they've seen everything
stagnating on enhancements, no AI features
static documents not LMS, separate licensing fee
manual long-form quiz grading, no adaptive pathing

ask_yourself_prompts: List every place L&D data lives today. Who owns the consolidation? Same person evaluating the LMS?
Is your platform being asked to fix a program-design problem? If yes, the platform won't be the answer.
Who's still in the room 90 days after the contract is signed? That's your real committee.
ask_next_vendor_prompts: Show me a report distinguishing active learners from inactive in the last 30 days. Branch by branch.
If we leave you in 3 years, what data comes out, in what format, with what metadata?
Show me last-updated dates on every course. What's the refresh SLA for security, AWS, compliance?
Can a direct manager assign a course to their team without going through L&D admin? Show me the manager view.

pricing_pattern.common_mistake: Compared current LMS spend against new LMS + content bundle (apples to oranges)
pricing_pattern.what_they_should_compare: Current LMS + content stack (LinkedIn Learning, OpenSesame, Skillsoft, GoOne) vs new bundle
pricing_pattern.advice: Before any pricing conversation, pull a separate line-item for what content costs today.

evaluation_committee.common_pattern: Most bring 2+ stakeholders. Day after signing, it's usually 1 person running it.

avoid_framing: feature-first messaging without a 'why should I care'
LMS-centric framing (frame business outcome, not platform)
engagement metrics as outcomes (board wants revenue/retention impact)
promising 'all-in-one' without naming the specific incumbents being replaced
demoing 'general overview' (the buyers who pre-loaded specific asks got demos that landed)
